summary: Solid, comprehensive contract with 30 testable criteria covering all 5 state management functions. All criteria have specific test steps and measurable thresholds. Regression coverage for sprints 1 and 2 is correct. Accepting with minor advisory notes below.

criteriaAnalysis.testability: PASS -- All 30 criteria have concrete testMethod, testSteps, and threshold fields. Every criterion can be objectively verified by an evaluator. Test steps specify exact assertions (e.g., 'asserts .completedSprints contains sprint 1 exactly once').
criteriaAnalysis.completeness: PASS -- All 5 functions from sprint plan F5 are covered: init_harness_state (C3-03 through C3-09, 7 criteria), log_cost (C3-10 through C3-12, 3 criteria), update_progress (C3-13 through C3-16, 4 criteria), update_handoff (C3-17 through C3-22, 6 criteria), update_regression_registry (C3-23 through C3-26, 4 criteria). Structural criteria (C3-01, C3-02), integration (C3-27), quality (C3-28), and regression (C3-29, C3-30) round out the contract.
criteriaAnalysis.regression: PASS -- regressionSprints correctly lists [1, 2]. C3-29 covers sprint 1 infrastructure tests. C3-30 covers sprint 2 pure function tests. Both are integration-level regression checks via run.sh.
criteriaAnalysis.outOfScope: PASS -- Correctly defers git tests (sprint 4), hook tests (sprint 5), edge cases (sprint 6). Correctly forbids modifications to existing harness source and prior sprint deliverables. Reasonable exclusion of check_cost_cap (no testable state mutation).

advisoryNotes.issue: Missing MAX_CONTRACT_ROUNDS from tested environment variables. The source code at utils.sh:82 supports 6 env vars: MODEL, CONTEXT_STRATEGY, MAX_SPRINT_ATTEMPTS, MAX_CONTRACT_ROUNDS, COST_CAP_PER_SPRINT, TOTAL_COST_CAP. C3-08 description only lists 5 (omits MAX_CONTRACT_ROUNDS). Generator should ideally test all 6, but the criterion description uses 'e.g.' in testSteps, leaving room for the generator to include it.
advisoryNotes.recommendation: Generator should test MAX_CONTRACT_ROUNDS alongside the 5 listed vars. Evaluator will not fail C3-08 for this omission but will note it in quality scoring.

advisoryNotes.issue: No criterion explicitly tests timestamp presence in update_progress output. Product spec F5 says 'Includes status, attempt, timestamp' but C3-14 only verifies status and attempt. The source (utils.sh:163) writes a '**Time**:' field. Testing exact timestamp values is impractical, but asserting the presence of a '**Time**:' line or ISO-8601 pattern would improve coverage.
advisoryNotes.recommendation: Generator may include timestamp presence assertion within C3-14 tests. Not blocking since timestamps are non-deterministic and the core behavior (status, attempt, sprint name, merge SHA) is well-covered.

advisoryNotes.issue: Criterion says 'default values (model=opus, contextStrategy=reset, maxSprintAttempts=3, etc.)' but does not enumerate all defaults. The actual defaults from source are: model=opus, contextStrategy=reset, maxSprintAttempts=3, maxContractRounds=3, costCapPerSprint=25.00, totalCostCap=200.00. The 'etc.' is ambiguous.
advisoryNotes.recommendation: Generator should test all 6 default values. Evaluator will verify the test checks at minimum the 3 explicitly listed defaults.

advisoryNotes.issue: C3-26 tests 'contract with no criteria array' but the test steps describe a fixture with '.criteria as empty array'. These are semantically different: an empty array [] vs a missing .criteria field entirely. The jq expression in update_regression_registry (utils.sh:230) would handle [] fine but could error on a truly missing .criteria field. The empty-array interpretation is the correct one to test here; the missing-field case is an edge case appropriate for sprint 6.
advisoryNotes.recommendation: No action needed. The empty-array interpretation in the test steps is correct for this sprint's scope.

init_harness_state.coverage: Comprehensive. Tests output files, content correctness, env var overrides, and defaults.

log_cost.coverage: Adequate. Tests correct entry structure, missing-field resilience, and accumulation.

update_progress.coverage: Good. Tests sprint name resolution, status/attempt, merge SHA presence/absence.

update_handoff.coverage: Excellent. Tests creation, update, idempotency, field preservation, and git fields.

update_regression_registry.coverage: Good. Tests happy path, missing contract, empty contract, empty criteria array.
